claim: Amyloid-directed monoclonal antibodies (anti-Abeta mAbs) carry a class boxed warning for amyloid-related imaging abnormalities (ARIA-E edema, ARIA-H microhemorrhage), which can be serious/fatal — exemplified by lecanemab (Leqembi).
faers_caveat: FAERS = spontaneous reports; reporting bias, no denominator — not incidence
rems_or_action: No REMS; boxed warning; baseline+periodic MRI for ARIA; ApoE4 genotyping recommended pre-initiation
trial_vs_realworld: FAERS confirms ARIA-E/ARIA-H among the top post-launch terms, consistent with the labeled (trial-derived) liability
quote: WARNING: AMYLOID RELATED IMAGING ABNORMALITIES Monoclonal antibodies directed against aggregated forms of beta amyloid, including LEQEMBI, can cause amyloid related imaging abnormalities (ARIA), characterized as ARIA with edema (ARIA-E) and ARIA with hemosiderin deposition (ARIA-H).

claim: NMDA-antagonist antidepressants (esketamine/Spravato) carry a boxed warning for sedation, dissociation, respiratory depression, abuse/misuse, plus the antidepressant-class suicidality risk — managed under a REMS.
boxed_warning: sedation; dissociation; respiratory depression; abuse and misuse; suicidal thoughts and behaviors
faers_caveat: FAERS = spontaneous reports; reporting bias, no denominator
rems_or_action: SPRAVATO REMS — restricted distribution; administered only in certified settings with post-dose monitoring
trial_vs_realworld: Labeled acute effects (dissociation, sedation) are the dominant FAERS terms post-market — consistent with the boxed warning
quote: WARNING: SEDATION; DISSOCIATION; RESPIRATORY DEPRESSION; ABUSE AND MISUSE; and SUICIDAL THOUGHTS AND BEHAVIORS

claim: Systemic AAV gene therapies (onasemnogene abeparvovec/Zolgensma) carry a class hepatotoxicity liability — acute serious liver injury and fatal acute liver failure — mandating corticosteroid prophylaxis and liver monitoring.
boxed_warning: serious liver injury and acute liver failure (fatal cases reported)
faers_caveat: FAERS = spontaneous reports; reporting bias, no denominator
rems_or_action: No REMS; boxed warning; pre-infusion liver assessment + systemic corticosteroid before/after + >=3 months liver monitoring
trial_vs_realworld: FAERS AST/ALT/hepatic-enzyme increases corroborate the labeled hepatotoxicity; fatal acute liver failure was a post-approval finding reflected in the boxed warning
quote: WARNING: SERIOUS LIVER INJURY and ACUTE LIVER FAILURE Cases of acute liver failure with fatal outcomes have been reported.

claim: Serotonergic (5-HT2) agents like fenfluramine (Fintepla, a Dravet/LGS antiseizure drug) carry a cardiac valvulopathy + pulmonary-arterial-hypertension liability requiring serial echocardiography under a REMS.
boxed_warning: valvular heart disease and pulmonary arterial hypertension
faers_caveat: FAERS = spontaneous reports; reporting bias, no denominator
rems_or_action: FINTEPLA REMS — prescriber/pharmacy certification + mandatory baseline and periodic echocardiograms
trial_vs_realworld: The labeled valvulopathy liability is mirrored in FAERS by tricuspid + mitral valve incompetence among the top terms post-market
quote: WARNING: VALVULAR HEART DISEASE and PULMONARY ARTERIAL HYPERTENSION FINTEPLA can cause valvular heart disease and pulmonary arterial hypertension

claim: Vigabatrin (Sabril/Vigadrone, a refractory-epilepsy/infantile-spasms drug) carries a class-defining boxed warning for permanent, often-irreversible bilateral concentric visual-field constriction — under a REMS.
boxed_warning: permanent vision loss (bilateral concentric visual-field constriction)
faers_caveat: FAERS = spontaneous; the vision-loss liability is UNDER-captured in FAERS (insidious/gradual) — it rests on the trial/label record, not FAERS frequency
rems_or_action: Vigabatrin REMS — restricted distribution; periodic vision testing required
trial_vs_realworld: Top FAERS terms are dominated by efficacy-loss/seizure, NOT vision loss — a key reminder that low FAERS counts do not mean low risk for insidious harms
quote: WARNING: PERMANENT VISION LOSS VIGADRONE can cause permanent bilateral concentric visual field constriction, including tunnel vision that can result in disability.

claim: Antidepressants as a class carry a boxed suicidality warning — increased risk of suicidal thoughts/behavior in children, adolescents, and young adults (SSRI exemplar: fluoxetine).
faers_caveat: FAERS = spontaneous; the labeled suicidality signal is not among the top FAERS terms (high-volume use; reporting bias, no denominator)
rems_or_action: No REMS; class boxed warning; monitor for clinical worsening / emergent suicidality, esp. at initiation and dose changes
trial_vs_realworld: Boxed suicidality risk derives from short-term controlled pediatric/young-adult pooled trial analyses, not FAERS frequency
quote: WARNING: SUICIDAL THOUGHTS AND BEHAVIORS Antidepressants increased the risk of suicidal thoughts and behavior in children, adolescents, and young adults in short-term studies.

claim: Cannabidiol (Epidiolex, a Dravet/LGS/TSC antiseizure drug) carries a Warnings-and-Cautions hepatotoxicity liability (transaminase elevations), amplified by concomitant valproate/clobazam — no boxed warning.
class_mechanism: plant-derived cannabidiol antiseizure medication (Dravet, LGS, TSC)
boxed_warning: NONE — hepatotoxicity is in Warnings & Cautions, not a boxed warning
faers_source: openFDA FAERS event API (generic_name=cannabidiol; note broad off-label CBD reporting)
faers_caveat: FAERS = spontaneous; hepatic terms are not among the top — under-represented; broad cannabidiol query captures off-label use
rems_or_action: No REMS; Warnings & Cautions hepatotoxicity; baseline + periodic transaminases/bilirubin; dose-adjust with valproate/clobazam
trial_vs_realworld: Hepatotoxicity is a labeled/trial finding under-represented in top FAERS terms (efficacy/GI/somnolence dominate)
source: openFDA drug/label API (warnings_and_cautions — no boxed warning)
quote: EPIDIOLEX can cause transaminase elevations. Concomitant use of valproate and higher doses of EPIDIOLEX increase the risk of transaminase elevations.

claim: Antisense oligonucleotides (nusinersen/Spinraza, intrathecal SMA ASO) carry ASO-class Warnings for thrombocytopenia/coagulation abnormalities and renal toxicity; intrathecal delivery adds lumbar-puncture risks — no boxed warning.
boxed_warning: NONE — thrombocytopenia + renal toxicity are ASO-class Warnings & Cautions
faers_caveat: FAERS = spontaneous; top terms reflect the intrathecal-administration burden (post-LP syndrome, procedural pain), not the labeled hematologic/renal liabilities
rems_or_action: No REMS; Warnings & Cautions; baseline+periodic platelet/coagulation + urine-protein (renal) monitoring
trial_vs_realworld: The ASO-class thrombocytopenia/renal warnings are label/trial-derived; FAERS is dominated by the intrathecal procedure burden instead
source: openFDA drug/label API (warnings_and_cautions — no boxed warning)
quote: Thrombocytopenia and Coagulation Abnormalities: Increased risk for bleeding complications; testing required at baseline and before each dose and as clinically needed
